transcript.speaker: doctor
transcript.text: Good morning. I'm Dr. Chen. What brings you in today?

transcript.speaker: patient
transcript.text: I've had chest pain for about two hours. It started suddenly while I was gardening.

transcript.speaker: doctor
transcript.text: Can you tell me your age and any medical history?

transcript.speaker: patient
transcript.text: I'm 67. I have hypertension — been on lisinopril for years.

transcript.speaker: patient
transcript.text: Yes, I take warfarin for a heart valve replacement.

timeline.summary: Patient reports acute chest pain, 2hr duration, exertional onset
Safety: Anticoagulation + chest pain — ACS workup indicated

safetyFlags.concern: Warfarin + chest pain — anticoagulation complicates ACS management
safetyFlags.rationale: Patient on warfarin presenting with chest pain and left arm radiation. Balance bleeding vs thrombotic risk.

soap.subjective: 67M with 2hr acute chest pain, exertional onset, left arm radiation, mild SOB. PMH: HTN, mechanical heart valve. Meds: lisinopril, warfarin.
soap.objective: Pending: ECG, troponin. Vitals not yet documented.
soap.assessment: Acute chest pain concerning for ACS in anticoagulated patient with cardiac history.
soap.plan: ECG, troponin, aspirin (consider bleeding risk), cardiology consult.

research.findings: Anticoagulated patients with ACS require careful balance of antithrombotic therapy. Warfarin may need reversal depending on bleeding risk.

citations.title: ACCF/AHA Guidelines — Anticoagulation in ACS
citations.snippet: Management of anticoagulated patients with ACS...

handoff.patientSummary: 67-year-old male with acute chest pain (2hr), HTN, mechanical heart valve on warfarin and lisinopril.
outstandingQuestions: Any prior cardiac interventions beyond valve replacement?
recommendedActions: Assess bleeding vs thrombotic risk before additional antiplatelet therapy
